meanings.definition: to cause to separate and go in different directions
examples: She waved her hand and scattered the crowds

meanings.definition: discontinue an association or relation; go different ways
examples: The business partners broke over a tax question
The couple separated after 25 years of marriage
My friend and I split up
We interrupt the program for the following messages

meanings.definition: cause to go into a solution
examples: The recipe says that we should dissolve a cup of sugar in two cups of water

meanings.definition: take apart into its constituent pieces
destroy the completeness of a set of related items
examples: The book dealer would not break the set

meanings.definition: attack with or as if with a pickaxe of ice or rocky ground, for example
close at the end of a session
bring the association of to an end or cause to break up
examples: The decree officially dissolved the marriage
the judge dissolved the tobacco company

meanings.definition: break or cause to break into pieces
separate (substances) into constituent elements or parts
